articles.title: vivo unveils premium TWS 5 Pro buds
articles.description: vivo just recently unveiled the X Fold6 in China, and alongside it, a new set of premium wireless buds. The vivo TWS 5 Pro buds look pretty unassuming on the surface, but actually deliver quite a few premium features. The earbuds feature a dual-driver acoustic setup consisting of a custom ultra-miniature balanced armature co-developed by vivo and Knowles, paired with a self-developed dual-ring dynamic driver. The two drivers are arranged in a coaxial acoustic architecture, aligning the acoustic centers of the high and low frequencies for improved coherence. According to vivo, the...

articles.title: Apple Vision Pro and Apple’s smart glasses executive is joining OpenAI
articles.description: OpenAI has reportedly hired Paul Meade, a key Apple executive who led engineering for the Vision Pro and the company's secret smart glasses project. Meade's move follows a significant hardware engineering shake-up at Apple, where he was reportedly demoted. His departure is seen as a major blow to Apple's future in spatial computing and wearables, as he is expected to spearhead OpenAI's new AI-first consumer devices.

articles.title: NASA looked back at the first Black Hole ever photographed, and found a big surprise
articles.description: The universe keeps reshaping our cosmic perspective. When the Event Horizon Telescope captured the historic first image of a black hole in 2019, humanity stared at a fiery orange ring. That glowing donut of hot gas became an instant scientific monument. However, static pictures rarely tell the whole story of deep space. New Chandra X-ray observations reveal moving jet activity inside the heart of the galaxy Messier 87, proving this monster is far from quiet.

Final decisions on objections will be binding.

articles.title: Casio G-Shock GA-2100CC-3A Review | Coca-Cola x G-Shock Watch
articles.description: At Business Today we recently got our hands on the vibrant, retro-styled G-Shock x Coca-Cola collaboration watch and decided to put its legendary durability to the ultimate workspace trial. Known for practically indestructible building watches, we wanted to see if this special edition piece could survive real-world chaos... so we dropped it straight from the second-floor of our office. Watch the full video to see if it survived. Merging classic glass bottle Coke aesthetics with the rugged DNA of G-Shock, this timepiece proves it is far more than just a slick collector's item. Would you ever trust your favorite watch to survive a sudden two-story plunge? Do you like the design of this watch? Tell us in the comments, share this video and follow Business Today TV for more.

articles.title: Forget coffee breaks: This Microsoft employee got a professional blow-dry in office during lunch break, and the internet wants the same perk
articles.description: A Microsoft employee's viral video showcasing a professional hair blow-dry during her lunch break at the Ireland office has sparked widespread envy online. The perk, highlighted as a way to make employees feel valued and refreshed, has many wishing for similar benefits at their own workplaces. The clip has even inspired some to consider applying for jobs at Microsoft, emphasizing the company's commitment to employee well-being beyond just work itself.

articles.title: Maharashtra bets on AI to solve Konkan’s oldest mystery
articles.description: Maharashtra has earmarked ₹15 crore for a four-year project that will use AI, drone imaging and archaeological research to analyse thousands of prehistoric petroglyphs across 107 Konkan villages, bolstering efforts to secure UNESCO World Heritage status for the sites.

articles.title: Apple starts selling refurbished MacBook Neo, bringing the price down to its pre-hike level
articles.description: Yesterday, Apple hiked the prices of all of its products except for iPhones, and the MacBook Neo didn't get spared - its price went up by $100 in the US. Today, Apple has started offering the MacBook Neo in its refurbished store, and the price for the entry-level model with 256GB of storage and no Touch ID is now back to $599. That used to be the price for the new laptop pre-hike, now you can get a refurbished one for the same amount. If you want the one with 512GB of storage and Touch ID, you'll have to pay $679, which is $20 less than this variant used to go for new before...

articles.title: US allows limited release of Anthropic Mythos
articles.description: WASHINGTON: The U.S. government on Friday allowed ​Anthropic to release its powerful ​Claude Mythos 5 AI model to some "trusted partners," according to ​a Commerce Department letter seen by Reuters.The action comes two weeks after the government ordered the AI firm to suspend access to some of its models, due to fears they could ‌be deployed ⁠by military ⁠intelligence users in China, Russia or other countries of concern.More than 100 companies and institutions will ​now have access to Mythos 5, including many Fortune 500 companies, a source familiar with ​the new directive said, declining to be identified due to the sensitivity of the matter.Also read | US judge won't immediately dismiss criminal charges against Gautam AdaniAnthropic and the White House did not immediately comment.Anthropic had abruptly disabled ​its most advanced AI models - Mythos 5 and ⁠Fable 5 - for ‌all users after the government's export control order."Since the ​issuance of my ​June 12 letter, Anthropic has worked with the U.S. government ⁠to address risks associated with the Covered Models. These ​efforts have yielded significant progress," U.S. Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick ​said in the letter to Anthropic.Also read | Mystery solved? HDFC finds no trace of truth in ex-chair's claimsLutnick added that an export license will no longer be needed for Claude Mythos 5 Model to trusted companies and their foreign national employees, or to Anthropic's foreign national employees but restrictions will remain in place for companies that are not on the approved list."In just ‌two weeks, we have worked diligently to ensure America remains the global leader in AI while safeguarding our security," a Commerce Department ​spokesperson said.The ​letter did not mention ⁠the status of Fable 5.The government is moving towards allowing Anthropic to release Fable as well, although a timeline is unclear, the source said.IPO-bound Anthropic's relationship with ​the U.S. government has been rocky this year. The company refused to allow the U.S. military to use its AI models for domestic surveillance and fully autonomous weapons systems and the government retaliated by putting it on a national security blacklist.

articles.title: West Asia conflict may delay India's chip projects
articles.description: India's ambitious semiconductor projects, including Tata Electronics' Dholera fab, face potential delays due to the West Asia conflict. Logistical snags have disrupted the supply of crucial speciality gases, chemicals, and metals, leading to concerns about cost overruns and extended timelines. While some believe disruptions have eased, experts warn of significant pushes to production schedules, impacting both construction and operational phases.

articles.title: "A Ted Bundy vibe": ChudTheBuilder rocked by explosive new allegations as YouTuber BabyJayne releases alleged photos and private messages
articles.description: Jailed streamer ChudTheBuilder is facing fresh allegations after YouTuber BabyJayne claimed they had a past intimate relationship and released alleged photos and private messages. She accused him of making misogynistic remarks and described his behavior as intense during their time together. BabyJayne's claims have not been independently verified, and ChudTheBuilder has not publicly responded. He remains in custody after a judge denied his request to lower his $1 million bond.

articles.title: Samsung gets sued by another patent troll trying to make a quick buck
articles.description: Yet another patent troll wants to get paid by Samsung. Tau Ceti Ventures LLC has filed a lawsuit against Samsung in the US District Court for the Eastern District of Texas, alleging that the company infringed upon ten of its patents relating to making displays brighter, preventing screen burn-in, improving battery life, and reducing manufacturing costs. Samsung allegedly used the infringing display technology in the Galaxy S25, Galaxy Z Flip7, Galaxy Watch8, Galaxy Tab A9+, Galaxy Book4, and a whole range of smart TVs and monitors. Tau Ceti claims that Samsung was aware of the patents or...
